what: When lines converge at the right end
title: Comparing topics over time
text: Direct labels legible overlap, topics sometimes end period nearly share. plot_scopus_comparison() spreads converging labels apart automatically, point figure actually drawn, stay readable figure size rather stacking unreadable pile. six sub-areas materials-science research end 2013–2021 within three points one another.  Without , six labels ending within three points print top one another; every one still readable, still joined line short leader.
code: years <- 2013:2021 ends <- c(18, 18.6, 19.2, 19.8, 20.4, 21) names(ends) <- c("graphene", "perovskites", "MXenes", "COFs", "MOFs", "aerogels") ref_n <- round(seq(500, 2000, length.out = length(years))) converge <- function(end) round(end * (0.5 + 0.5 * (0:(length(years) - 1)) / (length(years) - 1)) * ref_n / 100) counts <- lapply(ends, converge)  cmp_converging <- tibble::tibble(   query = "q",   query_type = c(rep("reference", length(years)),                  rep("comparison", length(counts) * length(years))),   abridged_query = c(rep("energy materials", length(years)),                      rep(names(counts), each = length(years))),   year = rep(years, length(counts) + 1),   n = c(ref_n, unlist(counts, use.names = FALSE)),   reference_n = rep(ref_n, length(counts) + 1),   comparison_percentage = 100 * c(ref_n, unlist(counts, use.names = FALSE)) /     rep(ref_n, length(counts) + 1),   average_comparison_percentage = c(rep(100, length(years)),                                     rep(ends, each = length(years))) ) class(cmp_converging) <- c("scopus_comparison", class(cmp_converging)) plot_scopus_comparison(cmp_converging)

title: Designing precise queries across disciplines
text: builder serves field. call returns exact query string sent ‘Scopus’. last example uses AUTHKEY, author-supplied keywords, isolates work self-identifies field cuts incidental mentions.
code: scopus_query("CRISPR", .field = "TITLE-ABS-KEY")              # molecular biology #> [1] "TITLE-ABS-KEY(CRISPR)" scopus_query("gravitational waves", .field = "TITLE-ABS-KEY") # physics #> [1] "TITLE-ABS-KEY(gravitational waves)" scopus_query("microplastics", .field = "TITLE-ABS-KEY")       # environmental science #> [1] "TITLE-ABS-KEY(microplastics)" scopus_query("blockchain", .field = "TITLE-ABS-KEY")          # computer science #> [1] "TITLE-ABS-KEY(blockchain)" scopus_query("digital humanities", .field = "AUTHKEY")        # humanities #> [1] "AUTHKEY(digital humanities)"

title: Designing precise queries across disciplines
text: Passing several terms joins . default operator , available .op.
code: # Two concepts that must co-occur (materials science). scopus_query("perovskite", "solar cell", .field = "TITLE-ABS-KEY") #> [1] "TITLE-ABS-KEY(perovskite) AND TITLE-ABS-KEY(solar cell)"  # Spelling variants, either of which will do (economics). scopus_query("behavioral economics", "behavioural economics", .op = "OR") #> [1] "behavioral economics OR behavioural economics"  # A family of related tools (molecular biology). scopus_query("CRISPR", "Cas9", "Cas12", .op = "OR") #> [1] "CRISPR OR Cas9 OR Cas12"

what: From a query to a plan
title: Designing precise queries across disciplines
text: composed query drops straight rest workflow. anchors year-partitioned plan, keeps cell API’s 5000-record ceiling. plan ready size run, contacts API.
code: q <- scopus_query("gut microbiome", "immunology", .field = "TITLE-ABS-KEY") q #> [1] "TITLE-ABS-KEY(gut microbiome) AND TITLE-ABS-KEY(immunology)" plan <- scopus_plan(q, years = 2015:2022, partition = "year") plan #> <scopus_plan> (8 cells, view "STANDARD", partition "year") #> # A tibble: 8 × 6 #>    cell query                                        date   year view  page_size #> * <int> <chr>                                        <chr> <int> <chr>     <int> #> 1     1 TITLE-ABS-KEY(gut microbiome) AND TITLE-ABS… 2015   2015 STAN…       200 #> 2     2 TITLE-ABS-KEY(gut microbiome) AND TITLE-ABS… 2016   2016 STAN…       200 #> 3     3 TITLE-ABS-KEY(gut microbiome) AND TITLE-ABS… 2017   2017 STAN…       200 #> 4     4 TITLE-ABS-KEY(gut microbiome) AND TITLE-ABS… 2018   2018 STAN…       200 #> 5     5 TITLE-ABS-KEY(gut microbiome) AND TITLE-ABS… 2019   2019 STAN…       200 #> 6     6 TITLE-ABS-KEY(gut microbiome) AND TITLE-ABS… 2020   2020 STAN…       200 #> 7     7 TITLE-ABS-KEY(gut microbiome) AND TITLE-ABS… 2021   2021 STAN…       200 #> 8     8 TITLE-ABS-KEY(gut microbiome) AND TITLE-ABS… 2022   2022 STAN…       200 scopus_count(q, years = 2015:2022) records <- scopus_fetch_plan(plan)

title: Author keywords and references
text: Search API’s COMPLETE view carries authkeywords field alongside usual title, DOI date. Requesting costs extra request beyond COMPLETE’s smaller page size (25 records per page, 200 STANDARD), already means requests, quota, number records. development, field came back NA even live, otherwise fully-entitled key, documents carry author keywords ‘Scopus’ , points entitlement gap specific one field rather documents genuinely none. keywords come back NA, worth raising Scopus/Elsevier account contact.
code: recs <- scopus_fetch("DOI(10.1038/nature14539)", view = "COMPLETE") recs$authkeywords

title: Author keywords and references
text: reference list available Search view; needs Abstract Retrieval’s FULL REF view, entitlement separate ordinary abstract access Search access. per-document endpoint, retrieving references n documents costs n requests Abstract Retrieval’s , smaller weekly quota, separate Search’s. view = "FULL" recommended default: development, returned complete, correctly counted reference list every document tried, view = "REF" returned inconsistent, sometimes-truncated subset, otherwise identical request made moments apart. scopus_abstract() warns number references returned match document’s reported count, rather returning partial list silently. key subscription tier cover requested view raises scopus_error_forbidden condition naming view, rather generic HTTP failure, stops whole batch rather repeating failure every remaining identifier, since entitlement account-level property, per-document one. handful identifiers, pass cache_dir interrupted quota-limited batch resumes without re-spending quota already spent:
code: ab <- scopus_abstract(   "10.1038/nature14539",   view = "FULL", include = c("references", "keywords") ) ab$references[[1]][, c("title", "authors", "source", "year")] attr(ab, "n_requests")   # requests spent so far attr(ab, "quota")$remaining ab <- scopus_abstract(   dois, view = "FULL", include = c("references", "keywords"),   cache_dir = "abstract-cache" )

what: Why page size is a quota decision
title: Search plans and quota-aware retrieval
text: Scopus charges quota per request, per record. page may hold 200 records STANDARD view, 25 COMPLETE. Retrieving thousand records pages 200 therefore costs five requests, pages 25 cost forty. reason page_size defaults largest view allows, efficiency rscopus relies , sense evasion quota: every request counted, 5000-record ceiling still holds.

title: A Reproducible Workflow Layer for Scopus Bibliographic Searches
text: key configured, pieces compose real retrieval. calls contact API consume quota, run :
code: # 1. Compose a field-tagged query, then a reproducible plan partitioned by #    year to stay under the API's start < 5000 ceiling. q <- scopus_query("perovskite", "solar cell", .field = "TITLE-ABS-KEY") plan <- scopus_plan(q, years = 2012:2022, partition = "year")  # 2. Size it before spending quota. scopus_count(q, years = 2012:2022)  # 3. Execute, caching each year so an interrupted run can resume. records <- scopus_fetch_plan(plan, cache_dir = scopus_cache_dir(), resume = TRUE)  # 4. Save a clean DOI list, or export the records for a reference manager #    (Zotero, EndNote, Mendeley) or a LaTeX bibliography. scopus_extract_dois(records, file = file.path(tempdir(), "dois.csv")) as_bibtex(records, file = file.path(tempdir(), "records.bib")) as_ris(records, file = file.path(tempdir(), "records.ris"))  # 5. Compare how a method spreads across application areas over time. cmp <- scopus_compare_topics(   reference_query  = "deep learning",   comparison_terms = c("computer vision", "drug discovery", "medical imaging"),   years            = 2013:2022,   field            = "TITLE-ABS-KEY" ) plot_scopus_comparison(cmp)  # 6. Read the abstract of a known record, or harvest a whole large query past #    the 5000-record ceiling with cursor pagination. scopus_abstract("10.1038/s41586-019-0001-1") all_records <- scopus_fetch("TITLE-ABS-KEY(microplastics)", cursor = TRUE)

text: coercion methods return plain tibble data frame columns scopus_records class removed. autoplot() method returns ggplot2::ggplot records per year. tibble class scopus_records columns entry_number (integer), scopus_id (character), doi (character), title (character), authors (character, creator names joined "; " several listed), year (integer, leading four digits cover date), date (character, ISO cover date), publication (character, source title), citations (integer) query (character). missing field becomes NA, empty result set yields zero-row tibble columns. view = "COMPLETE", authkeywords column added: author-supplied keywords 'Scopus' Search API returns view, single string 'Scopus' " | "-delimited form (NA document none, API omits field given key's entitlement; see Details). is_scopus_records() returns length-one logical.

Even COMPLETE view, 'Scopus' API keys return populated author keywords (observed directly live, otherwise fully-entitled key development, documents carry author keywords 'Scopus' ); keywords come back NA, field likely gated account's entitlement rather genuinely absent, worth raising 'Scopus'/Elsevier account contact.

title: Normalise raw 'Scopus' entries to a stable tidy schema — as_tibble.scopus_records
code: # A minimal entry as the API would return it. raw <- list(entry = list(   list(     `dc:identifier` = "SCOPUS_ID:1",     `prism:doi` = "10.1000/abc",     `dc:title` = "An example",     `dc:creator` = "Doe J.",     `prism:publicationName` = "Journal of Examples",     `prism:coverDate` = "2020-05-01",     `citedby-count` = "7"   ) )) scopus_records(raw, query = "TITLE(example)") #> <scopus_records> 1 record #> query: "TITLE(example)" #> # A tibble: 1 × 9 #>   entry_number scopus_id doi     title authors  year date  publication citations #>          <int> <chr>     <chr>   <chr> <chr>   <int> <chr> <chr>           <int> #> 1            1 1         10.100… An e… Doe J.   2020 2020… Journal of…         7  # A COMPLETE-view entry carrying author keywords. raw_complete <- list(entry = list(   list(     `dc:identifier` = "SCOPUS_ID:2",     `prism:doi` = "10.1000/def",     `dc:title` = "A second example",     authkeywords = "graphene | supercapacitor | energy storage"   ) )) scopus_records(raw_complete, view = "COMPLETE") #> <scopus_records> 1 record #> # A tibble: 1 × 11 #>   entry_number scopus_id doi     title authors  year date  publication citations #>          <int> <chr>     <chr>   <chr> <chr>   <int> <chr> <chr>           <int> #> 1            1 2         10.100… A se… NA         NA NA    NA                 NA #> # ℹ 2 more variables: query <chr>, authkeywords <chr>
